text:
User: ok, so I thikn we should remove it from the guide (no labels, etc) and also not delete the agent-derived decisions (I still think we should mark them though) [...] User: A = review filter
B = keep everything -- incuding when the user corrects the model explicitly [...] 
- **Capture everything** — user and agent claims both admitted; nothing dropped at the gate.
- **Authorship tag = internal metadata only.** Never rendered in guide prose → the 81%-dilution problem is gone by construction. [...] User: ok, so I thikn we should remove it from the guide (no labels, etc) and also not delete the agent-derived decisions (I still think we should mark them though)
 [...] 2. **Authorship becomes metadata, not prose.** Each claim still carries a user-vs-agent "mark," but at the data layer (on the claim/citation record), invisible in the rendered guide. That's the "still mark them."
3. **Supersession no longer deletes agent-derived claims.** When something is contradicted/superseded, the old claim is *kept as history* regardless of who authored it — same treatment as user claims. So retention becomes **symmetric** (keep the history either way); the only difference agent-vs-user makes is the recorded mark. [...] 
- **Capture everything** — user and agent claims both admitted; nothing dropped at the gate.
- **Authorship tag = internal metadata only.** Never rendered in guide prose → the 81%-dilution problem is gone by construction.
- **Nothing is ever deleted** — keep-all, *including when you explicitly correct the model*. Supersession keeps the old claim in the log (marked superseded + origin); the guide shows the live tip. [...] User: A = review filter [...] - **The tag's only job = a review filter** — a `wiki doctor`/audit pass surfaces agent-derived claims (esp. never-user-confirmed) for you to audit. No rendering, no deletion, no promotion lifecycle.
- This deliberately **sidesteps** the fact-vs-proposal classification — we never have to decide which agent claims are "soft," because we neither render nor delete by the tag.
